description: Add comprehensive unit tests and E2E tests for the open-tab cycling feature (bg:openPluginTab handler). The handler has a critical bug fix (tab-ID-based cycling instead of index-based) that must be regression-tested. Also add unit tests for the pickNextTab helper and handleBgOpenPluginTab handler in background-message-handlers.test.ts.

userStories.title: Unit tests for pickNextTab and handleBgOpenPluginTab in background-message-handlers.test.ts
userStories.description: Add comprehensive unit tests covering the open-tab cycling logic: pickNextTab helper function and the handleBgOpenPluginTab message handler. Test all edge cases including single tab, multiple tabs, first-click behavior (skips active tab), round-robin cycling, tab closed mid-cycle, no matching tabs with homepage fallback, and no matching tabs without homepage.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: pickNextTab with empty array returns undefined
Test: pickNextTab first call (no lastFocusedTabId) picks first non-active tab
Test: pickNextTab first call when all tabs are inactive picks the first tab
Test: pickNextTab first call when only one tab exists and it is active picks that tab
Test: pickNextTab cycles through tabs in stable tab-ID order after first call
Test: pickNextTab wraps around to the first tab after reaching the end
Test: pickNextTab handles lastFocusedTabId pointing to a tab that no longer exists (removed mid-cycle) — falls back to next in order
Test: handleBgOpenPluginTab returns { opened: false } when pluginName is missing
Test: handleBgOpenPluginTab returns { opened: false } when plugin meta not found
Test: handleBgOpenPluginTab with one matching tab calls chrome.tabs.update and chrome.windows.update
Test: handleBgOpenPluginTab with two tabs cycles between them on repeated calls (not always the same tab)
Test: handleBgOpenPluginTab with no matching tabs and homepage calls chrome.tabs.create with the homepage URL
Test: handleBgOpenPluginTab with no matching tabs and no homepage returns { opened: false }
Test: handleBgOpenPluginTab first call with active tab among matches focuses a non-active tab
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/browser-extension/src/background-message-handlers.test.ts (add tests)
- platform/browser-extension/src/background-message-handlers.ts (may need to export pickNextTab and TabEntry for testing, or test indirectly through handleBgOpenPluginTab)

Context:
The test file (background-message-handlers.test.ts) already has ~65 tests with a comprehensive mock setup. It mocks chrome.tabs.query, chrome.tabs.update, chrome.windows.update, chrome.tabs.create, chrome.windows.getLastFocused, and all storage APIs.

The key functions to test:

1. `pickNextTab(sorted: TabEntry[], pluginName: string): TabEntry | undefined` — a module-level function that selects the next tab to focus. It reads from the `lastFocusedTabId` Map (module-level state).

2. `handleBgOpenPluginTab` — the message handler that:
   - Gets plugin meta from storage
   - Calls `findAllMatchingTabs(meta)` to get current tabs
   - Filters to tabs with IDs, sorts by tab ID (stable order)
   - Calls `pickNextTab` to select the target
   - Calls `chrome.tabs.update` + `chrome.windows.update` to focus it
   - Falls back to `chrome.tabs.create({ url: homepage })` if no tabs and homepage exists
   - Returns `{ opened: false }` if no tabs and no homepage

The `lastFocusedTabId` Map and `pickNextTab` are module-level (not exported). Testing must go through `handleBgOpenPluginTab` or the test must import them. Check the existing test patterns — the test file uses `vi.importActual` and dynamic imports to access module internals.

Existing mock setup (background-message-handlers.test.ts):
- `mockGetPluginMeta` — returns a PluginMeta object
- `mockFindAllMatchingTabs` — returns chrome.tabs.Tab[]
- `mockTabsUpdate` — mock for chrome.tabs.update
- `mockTabsCreate` — mock for chrome.tabs.create
- `mockWindowsUpdate` — mock for chrome.windows.update

The tests should mock `findAllMatchingTabs` to return specific tab configurations, then call the message handler and verify the correct tab is focused.

Critical regression test: With 2 tabs where tab A is active, verify that:
1. First click focuses tab B (not tab A which is already active)
2. Second click focuses tab A
3. Third click focuses tab B

This is the exact bug that was fixed — the old code always focused the active tab first because findAllMatchingTabs sorts by rank.

Another edge case: When the previously focused tab is closed (its ID no longer in the sorted list), the `findIndex` returns -1, so `(-1 + 1) % length = 0` — it correctly falls back to the first tab. Test this.

Constraints:
- Follow the existing test patterns in the file (describe blocks, mock setup, async handlers)
- Do NOT modify the source code — only add tests
- Reset `lastFocusedTabId` state between tests if possible (check if the module exposes a reset function, or re-import the module for each test group)

userStories.title: E2E test for open-tab cycling with multiple tabs
userStories.description: Add an E2E test that verifies clicking the plugin icon in the side panel cycles through multiple matching tabs correctly. The first click should NOT focus the currently-active tab (the regression case). Each subsequent click should focus the next tab in stable order.
acceptanceCriteria: E2E test opens 2 test app tabs and verifies they are both recognized by the plugin
E2E test opens the side panel and waits for the plugin to show as ready
E2E test clicks the plugin icon and verifies it switches focus to a different tab (not the currently active one)
E2E test clicks the plugin icon again and verifies it switches to the other tab
E2E test clicks a third time and verifies it cycles back to the first tab
E2E test verifies tab focus changes by checking which tab is active after each click
Test properly cleans up all resources (tabs, server, extension) in try/finally
